methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Standard Subsea, a seed-stage ocean and maritime frontier-tech company founded by Christopher Amidon, has raised $10M+ to date. The comparable universe consists of three private peers in the same sector—Clippership, NetworkOcean, and Thalassa Robotics—with no direct public comps available for cross-validation.

A stage-progression methodology was applied, using the $10M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement. This approach yielded an implied valuation of $25M. The limited methodological toolkit reflects data constraints inherent to early-stage, private frontier-tech companies where revenue multiples and comparable transaction precedents are sparse or unavailable.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $21M (low) to $29M (high), with a median of $25M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of public comp benchmarks for the broader ocean and maritime sector, limited visibility into the three private peers' financials and growth trajectories, and the typical opacity of seed-stage capital structures and liquidation preferences. Future valuation refinement would benefit from additional transaction data within the maritime-tech subsector and clarity on Standard Subsea's revenue traction, unit economics, and technical differentiation relative to identified peers.
